summary: A Jest test in `test/config/get.ts` failed because `pnpm config get @my-org:registry` returned a URL with a trailing slash (`https://my-org.registry.example.com/`) while the test expected no trailing slash (`https://my-org.registry.example.com`). This caused 1 test suite to fail and the overall CI job to exit with code 1.
root_cause: The `pnpm config get` command now returns registry URLs with a trailing slash, but the test `pnpm config get reads npm options but ignores other settings from .npmrc` expects the URL without a trailing slash. The assertion `expect(stdout.toString().trim()).toBe('https://my-org.registry.example.com')` fails because the actual output is `'https://my-org.registry.example.com/'`.
relevant_tests: pnpm config get reads npm options but ignores other settings from .npmrc

evidence.reason: This is the exact assertion mismatch — the received value has a trailing slash that the test does not expect.

evidence.quote:
Test Suites: 1 failed, 3 skipped, 61 passed, 62 of 65 total
Tests:       1 failed, 9 skipped, 475 passed, 485 total
evidence.reason: Confirms exactly one test suite and one test failed.
Points to the exact line in the test file where the assertion fails.

suggested_fix: Update the test expectation at `test/config/get.ts:32` to accept a trailing slash (`.toBe('https://my-org.registry.example.com/')`) or, if the trailing slash is undesired behavior, fix the `pnpm config get` command to strip trailing slashes from registry URLs before outputting them.
